params.name: In the Wild North
params.description:
On June 23, 1891, Shishkin created a remarkable Russian winter landscape painting that would undoubtedly leave a significant mark in the history of art.
With a cold and stern color palette, Shishkin portrays cedar trees on distant northern cliffs. They stand tall and majestic, facing the silver moonlight. In fact, Shishkin's landscape paintings always embody a poetic atmosphere, and in the far north, he captures the unique lighting characteristics of moonlight. Through concise yet magnificent …
